definitions.text: Fashionable; popular; up to date; current.
examples: I think this band's sound is very now.

definitions.text: Used to introduce a point, a qualification of what has previously been said, a remonstration or a rebuke.
examples: Now, we all want what is best for our children.

definitions.text: Differently from the immediate past; differently from a more remote past or a possible future; differently from all other times.
examples: We all now want the latest toys for our children.

definitions.text: Since, because, in light of the fact.
examples: Now all the children have grown up and left, the house is very quiet.
Now that my sister has gotten rid of their cat, we can go to her house this coming Thanksgiving.
Now! Fire all we've got while the enemy is in reach!
There is no better time than now.

definitions.text: The state of not paying attention to the future or the past.
examples: She is living in the now.

definitions.text: A particular instant in time, as perceived at that instant.
